\begin{document}

\title{Representation-Dependent Harmonic Alignment in the UNESCO World Heritage Corpus:\\
Domed Monuments, Cluster Asymmetry, and Coordinate Choice}

\author{Seth Tenenbaum\thanks{ORCID: \href{https://orcid.org/0009-0008-5797-2498}{0009-0008-5797-2498}.
Correspondence: seth@fourthtemple.com}\\
Independent Scholar, USA}

\maketitle

\abstract{
We analyze UNESCO Cultural and Mixed World Heritage sites
($N=\NclusterTotal$) with a harmonic-alignment scoring framework. A global
sweep over frequency and phase selects a frame near 3\textdegree{} spacing
and the $\sim$35\textdegree{}E corridor. Subsequent analyses describe
longitude deviations within that selected frame. Weak but reproducible
structure appears across architectural subsets and related Eurasian
datasets. A Normalized Phasor Consensus (NPC) test shows that three
independently assembled monument corpora---UNESCO Cultural/Mixed, the UNESCO
domed-monument subset, and the Wikidata Q180987 stupa corpus---spontaneously
recover the same 3\textdegree{} anchor ($K=\npcMonK{}$, $p=\npcMonPermP{}$),
placing a consensus grid line within \npcMonGridDeltaE\textdegree{} of
35\textdegree{}E. A fourth corpus (OWTRAD Silk Road trade-route nodes) does
not share the anchor, confirming the convergence is specific to monument
corpora. The full-corpus Rayleigh periodogram is null
($p=\fullRayleighPermP$), correctly indicating the absence of a universal
global periodicity; this is compatible with the NPC result because Rayleigh
tests smooth sinusoidal density across the global distribution, diluted by
sites outside the Eurasian corridor, while NPC tests anchor recoverability
across independently assembled databases. The 3\textdegree{}/35\textdegree{}E
anchor is therefore a cross-corpus reproducible feature of Eurasian
monumental geography, not a universal spatial periodicity. The pipeline is
fully reproducible.
}

\bigskip

\noindent\textbf{Keywords:}
template alignment, spatial statistics, Levant, UNESCO World Heritage,
3-degree harmonics, representation dependence, circular statistics,
monument distribution, Mount Gerizim

\bigskip
